tasks.label: Play with REAL paddle (IMU): Build + Serve
tasks.detail: Hardware mode: build the UI, run the backend so the paddle's MPU6050 drives the on-screen paddle, route haptics to it, and open the browser. Hold the paddle still ~2 s at start to zero the gyro; press C in the browser to recenter 'flat'.

tasks.label: Play (mouse, no hardware): Build + Serve
tasks.detail: Build the UI, run the sim backend, and open the browser. Drive the paddle with the mouse — good fallback when the paddle isn't connected.

tasks.label: Play with REAL paddle (IMU + webcam position): Build + Serve
tasks.detail: Full 6-DOF: the IMU drives orientation and the webcam (MediaPipe hand tracking) drives position. Needs 'pip install mediapipe' and a webcam. Hold still ~2 s to zero the gyro; press C to recenter. If left/right is mirrored, set EDGEPONG_MP_X_SIGN=-1.

tasks.label: Calibrate: IMU axes
tasks.detail: Measures your paddle's pitch/yaw/roll and prints the EDGEPONG_IMU_AXES value to paste into the hardware task. Stop the backend first (this needs the telemetry port).

tasks.label: Fix: Kill stale backend (frees UDP 46000 / HTTP 8080)
tasks.detail: Run this if a launch fails to bind, or metrics show paddleConnected=false after a restart
args: $pids = @(); try { $pids += (Get-NetUDPEndpoint -LocalPort 46000 -ErrorAction Stop).OwningProcess } catch {}; try { $pids += (Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 8080 -State Listen -ErrorAction Stop).OwningProcess } catch {}; $pids = $pids | Sort-Object -Unique | Where-Object { $_ -gt 0 }; if ($pids) { $pids | ForEach-Object { Write-Host "Killing PID $_"; Stop-Process -Id $_ -Force } } else { Write-Host 'Nothing holding 46000/8080 — all clear.' }

tasks.label: REAL paddle: Serve + open browser
tasks.detail: helper — runs the hardware-mode backend, then opens the browser
dependsOn: Browser: Open when server is ready

tasks.label: IMU + webcam: Serve + open browser
tasks.detail: helper — runs hardware mode with MediaPipe position, then opens the browser
dependsOn: Browser: Open when server is ready

tasks.label: Mouse: Serve + open browser
tasks.detail: helper — runs the sim backend, then opens the browser
dependsOn: Browser: Open when server is ready

tasks.label: Backend: Run (real paddle IMU)
tasks.detail: helper — hardware mode: the real paddle's IMU drives orientation (position fixed), haptics auto-route back to it. Look for 'POSE SOURCE: real paddle IMU' in the log.

tasks.label: Backend: Run (IMU + webcam position)
tasks.detail: helper — hardware mode + MediaPipe: IMU drives orientation, webcam hand tracking drives position. Set EDGEPONG_MP_X_SIGN=-1 here if left/right is mirrored.

tasks.label: Backend: Run (sim)
tasks.detail: helper — game backend with mock camera + paddle on :8080 (mouse control)

tasks.label: Browser: Open when server is ready
tasks.detail: helper — waits for http://localhost:8080 (max 30 s), then opens it
args: $deadline = (Get-Date).AddSeconds(30); $up = $false; while ((Get-Date) -lt $deadline) { try { Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing -TimeoutSec 1 http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/health | Out-Null; $up = $true; break } catch { Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 500 } }; if ($up) { Write-Host 'Server up - opening browser'; Start-Process 'http://127.0.0.1:8080' } else { Write-Host 'Server did not come up within 30 s - not opening browser' }

tasks.label: Frontend: Build
tasks.detail: helper — typecheck + Vite production build into frontend/dist (served by the backend)
